Performance Evaluation of Data Balancing Techniques for Imbalanced Classification

This project investigates the impact of different data balancing techniques on imbalanced classification problems. The performance of a Random Forest classifier is evaluated using the original dataset, SMOTE oversampling, DBSMOTE oversampling, Random Undersampling, and Cost-Sensitive Random Forest without data preprocessing. The results are compared using multiple evaluation metrics to determine the most effective approach for handling class imbalance.

About Me

  • Computer Scientist with a focus on Automation Engineering, PLC Programming, and Data Analytics. I combine technical expertise with analytical problem-solving skills to develop practical and efficient solutions for industrial automation systems and data-driven applications.

  • Introduction:

    In this artical we will exploring performance measures for imbalance dataset "Card Fraud Detection" data, we will use the random forest classifier to detect credit frauds from the dataset, with the following specifications:
    • Baseline training with the original data.
    • SMOTE oversampling on the data.
    • DBSMOTE oversampling on the data.
    • Random undersampling on the data.
    • Without data preprocessing (oversampling or undersampling).

  • Exploring the data:

In [1]:
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split

df = pd.read_csv('data/creditcard.csv')

General variable & Functions:¶

In [4]:
accuracy_data = {'Accuracy'                     :[],
                 'Balanced accuracy'            :[],
                 'Precision'                    :[],
                 'Recall'                       :[],
                 'F1-Score'                     :[],
                 'G-Mean'                       :[],
                 'Kappa Score'                  :[],
                 'AUC score'                    :[],
                 'Average Precision Score (APS)':[],}
In [5]:
#Import scikit-learn metrics module for accuracy calculation
from sklearn.metrics import accuracy_score, balanced_accuracy_score, precision_score , recall_score, f1_score, cohen_kappa_score, roc_auc_score, average_precision_score 
from imblearn.metrics import geometric_mean_score

def accuracy_function(Case_Name , y_test_name , y_pred_name):
    print("     "+ Case_Name +"  ")
    print("--------------------------------------------------")
    # Model Accuracy:
    accuracy_score_name = accuracy_score(y_test_name , y_pred_name)
    accuracy_data['Accuracy'].append(accuracy_score_name)
    print("- Accuracy                     :", accuracy_score_name)
    print("--------------------------------------------------")
    # Model Balanced Accuracy:
    balanced_accuracy_name = balanced_accuracy_score(y_test_name , y_pred_name)
    accuracy_data['Balanced accuracy'].append(balanced_accuracy_name)
    print("- Balanced Accuracy            :", balanced_accuracy_name)
    print("--------------------------------------------------")
    # Model Precision Accuracy:
    precision_score_name = precision_score(y_test_name , y_pred_name)
    accuracy_data['Precision'].append(precision_score_name)
    print("- Precision                    :",precision_score_name)
    print("--------------------------------------------------")
    # Model Recall Accuracy:
    recall_score_name = recall_score(y_test_name , y_pred_name)
    accuracy_data['Recall'].append(recall_score_name)
    print("- Recall                       :",recall_score_name)
    print("--------------------------------------------------")
    # Model F1-Score Accuracy:
    f1_score_name = f1_score(y_test_name , y_pred_name)
    accuracy_data['F1-Score'].append(f1_score_name)
    print("- F1-Score                     :",f1_score_name)
    print("--------------------------------------------------")
    # Model G-Mean Accuracy:
    geometric_mean_score_name = geometric_mean_score(y_test_name , y_pred_name)
    accuracy_data['G-Mean'].append(geometric_mean_score_name)
    print("- G-Mean                       :",geometric_mean_score_name)
    print("--------------------------------------------------")
    # Model Kappa Score Accuracy:
    cohen_kappa_score_name = cohen_kappa_score(y_test_name , y_pred_name)
    accuracy_data['Kappa Score'].append(cohen_kappa_score_name)
    print("- Kappa Score                  :",cohen_kappa_score_name)
    print("--------------------------------------------------")
    # Model AUC score Accuracy:
    roc_auc_score_name = roc_auc_score(y_test_name , y_pred_name)
    accuracy_data['AUC score'].append(roc_auc_score_name)
    print("- AUC score                    :",roc_auc_score_name)
    print("--------------------------------------------------")
    # Model Average Precision Score (APS) Accuracy:
    average_precision_score_name = average_precision_score(y_test_name , y_pred_name)
    accuracy_data['Average Precision Score (APS)'].append(average_precision_score_name)
    print("- Average Precision Score (APS):",average_precision_score_name)
    print("--------------------------------------------------")
In [6]:
def drow_function_1(index , name):
    acc = list(accuracy_data.keys())
    values = [accuracy_data[i][index] for i in accuracy_data]

    fig = plt.figure(figsize = (10, 5))

    # creating the bar plot
    plt.bar(acc, values, color ='green',width = 0.4)

    plt.xlabel("performance measures")
    plt.ylabel("Accuracy")
    plt.title(name)
    plt.xticks(rotation=45)
    plt.show()
In [7]:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
def drow_function_2(ii):
    fig  = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(18, 5))
    ##########################################
    acc = ['AUC score:'                    + str(round(accuracy_data['AUC score'][ii]*100,2)),
           'Average Precision Score (APS):'+ str(round(accuracy_data['Average Precision Score (APS)'][ii]*100,2))]
    values = [accuracy_data['AUC score'][ii],accuracy_data['Average Precision Score (APS)'][ii]] 
    plt.bar(acc, values, color ='green',width = 0.4)
    plt.xlabel("performance measures")
    plt.ylabel("Accuracy")
    plt.title("AUC vs APS")
    plt.subplot(131)
    ##########################################
    acc = ['F1-Score:'+ str(round(accuracy_data['F1-Score'][ii]*100,2)),
           'Kappa Score:'+ str(round(accuracy_data['Kappa Score'][ii]*100,2))]
    values = [accuracy_data['F1-Score'][ii],accuracy_data['Kappa Score'][ii]] 
    plt.bar(acc, values, color ='red',width = 0.4)
    plt.xlabel("performance measures")
    plt.ylabel("Accuracy")
    plt.title("F1-Score VS Kappa Score")
    plt.subplot(132)
    ##########################################
    acc = ['F1-Score:'+ str(round(accuracy_data['F1-Score'][ii]*100,2)),
           'G-Mean:  '+ str(round(accuracy_data['G-Mean'][ii]*100,2))]
    values = [accuracy_data['F1-Score'][ii],accuracy_data['G-Mean'][ii]] 
    plt.bar(acc, values, color ='blue',width = 0.4)
    plt.xlabel("performance measures")
    plt.ylabel("Accuracy")
    plt.title("F1-Score vs G-Mean")
    plt.subplot(133)
    ###########################################
In [8]:
def Scatter_plot(x , y):
    c1 = plt.scatter(x[y==0]['Time'],x[y==0]["Amount"], marker='o', color='b')
    c2 = plt.scatter(x[y==1]['Time'],x[y==1]["Amount"], marker='o', color="y")
    plt.xlabel("Time")
    plt.ylabel("Amount")
    plt.title("Scatter points")

    plt.legend((c1,c2),
           ('Class 0', 'Class1'),
           scatterpoints=1,
           loc='upper center',
           ncol=3,
           fontsize=8)
    plt.show()

1- First case:

- Baseline training with the original data:

In [9]:
X1 = df.loc[:,df.columns!="Class"] # "Features"
y1 = df['Class']  # Labels

# Split dataset into training set and test set
X_train1, X_test1, y_train1, y_test1 = train_test_split(X1, y1, test_size=0.2  ) # Train Test split (80:20).
In [10]:
#Import Random Forest Model
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier
#Create a Gaussian Classifier
model1 = RandomForestClassifier()
#Train the model using the training sets.
model1.fit(X_train1,y_train1)

y_pred1 = model1.predict(X_test1)
In [11]:
accuracy_function("Baseline training with the original data" , y_test1, y_pred1)

In [12]:
# ROC Curve
from sklearn import metrics
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
y_pred_proba = model1.predict_proba(X_test1)[::,1]
fpr, tpr, _ = metrics.roc_curve(y_test1, y_pred1)
auc = metrics.roc_auc_score(y_test1, y_pred1)
plt.plot(fpr,tpr,label="data 1, auc="+str(auc))
plt.legend(loc=4)
plt.show()

# read the result dataframe
df_result_acc = pd.read_csv('results.csv')
df_result_acc.rename(columns = {'Unnamed: 0':'Case Study'}, inplace = True)
df_result_acc

  • ### Draw chart to compare each measure in all 5 cases:
In [217]:
def result_draw_func(measures):
    ax = df_result_acc.plot(x= 'Case Study', y= measures , kind="bar")
    ax.bar_label(ax.containers[0], labels=round(df_result_acc[measures]*100,2))
    ax.get_legend().remove()
    plt.xlabel("performance measures")
    plt.ylabel("Accuracy")
    plt.title(measures)
    plt.xticks(rotation=45)
    plt.show()
In [218]:
col = list(df_result_acc)[1:]
for i in col:
    result_draw_func(i)
  • ### F1-Score vs G-mean:
In [219]:
def compare_between_measures(measures_listb):
    ax = df_result_acc.plot(x= 'Case Study', y= measures_listb , kind="bar")
    ax.bar_label(ax.containers[0], labels=round(df_result_acc[measures_listb[0]]*100,2), color = "green",rotation=45)
    ax.bar_label(ax.containers[1], labels=round(df_result_acc[measures_listb[1]]*100,2), color = "red",rotation=45)
    plt.legend(loc='lower center')
    plt.xlabel("performance measures")
    plt.ylabel("Accuracy")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45)
    plt.show()
In [222]:
compare_between_measures(["F1-Score","G-Mean"])
  • ### F1-Score vs Kappa Score:
In [227]:
compare_between_measures(["F1-Score","Kappa Score"])
  • ### AUC score vs Average Precision Score (APS):
In [229]:
compare_between_measures(["AUC score","Average Precision Score (APS)"])
